body:
MORNING INTEL — 2026-07-13 (snapshot 2026-07-13_3)
Regime data: VIX 16.16 | HY OAS 270 | Breadth 72.7%
AMZN: NO_TRADE — IV Rank below minimum or missing (G2_iv_rank: observed None threshold >= 25.0)
NVDA: NO_TRADE — IV Rank below minimum or missing (G2_iv_rank: observed None threshold >= 25.0)
SPY: NO_TRADE — IV Rank below minimum or missing (G2_iv_rank: observed None threshold >= 25.0)
Portfolio risk after fills: pending Artha confirmation
Discipline: 30d system PnL $0 | override PnL $0
Data quality: issues: alpha_vantage_news

structured.takeaway: The current regime shows a moderately elevated VIX at 16.16 with a steep contango curve (9D 13.51 vs. 3M 19.16), signaling short-term volatility compression but longer-term risk premium remains elevated amid geopolitical tensions from Iran’s new strikes, which pressured the Nasdaq and S&P 500 lower at the open. SPY is under immediate downside pressure, trading at $751.78 with subdued implied volatility at 27.02% and negative sentiment (-1), reflecting cautious investor positioning. Conversely, NVDA at $208.96 and AMZN at $246.45 maintain strong bullish sentiment (+1) and elevated IV near 50%, fueled by structural growth drivers—AI infrastructure expansion for NVDA and MercadoLibre’s projection to join Amazon’s $50 billion revenue club for AMZN—suggesting these names remain key long candidates despite broad market jitters. The near-term catalyst to watch is the Treasury balance release today and the upcoming CPI/PPI data later this week, which could recalibrate risk appetite and bond yields (US10Y at 4.54%), potentially exacerbating market volatility or confirming inflationary pressures. A growing risk is the geopolitical escalation in the Middle East, which could derail the fragile risk-on environment and push volatility higher, especially if oil prices spike or supply chains are disrupted. System status: currently in warmup with IV rank gating inactive, so trade signals remain tentative.

news.title: Nasdaq and S&P 500 open lower as Iran launches new strikes
Prediction: MercadoLibre Will Join Amazon, Walmart, and Costco in the $50 Billion Revenue Club by 2027
Why Citigroup is the one to watch when banks report earnings this week
AI Infrastructure Boom Drives Taiwan Suppliers Toward U.S. Manufacturing Expansion
SK Hynix Makes its US Debut: Which ETFs Offer Exposure?
Economic calendar: Treasury balance today; CPI and PPI this week
India's inflation accelerates to 4.38% in June, exceeding forecasts
The stock-market rally now hinges more on AI than oil
Oil prices rise, stock futures dip after latest flare-up of U.S.-Iran strikes
Regular water isn't enough: The corporate gold rush for ‘functional hydration’
Fed Chair Warsh faces the hot seat as Congress presses for a read on the economy
‘Day-to-day dread’ haunts frustrated job seekers in era of low hiring
Job seekers giving up: Labor force participation rate falls to lowest in 50 years, outside of Covid era
China consumer price growth weakens in June while producer inflation rises to near 4-year high
U.S. job creation cools in June with payrolls growth of just 57,000; unemployment rate at 4.2%
World Cup could boost the June jobs report by 40,000, Goldman estimates
Core inflation rate hit 3.4% in May, highest since October 2023, Fed’s preferred gauge shows

narratives.this_week: Bank earnings dominate this week with BAC, WFC, JPM, GS, and MS reporting starting July 14-15. Investors will focus on credit quality and margin trends amid a stable VIX at 16.16 and elevated HY spreads near 270 bps, which suggest cautious risk sentiment. Watch for any signs of stress or resilience in financials that could ripple into broader equity sentiment, particularly impacting SPY given the sector’s weight, while NVDA and AMZN remain less directly affected.
narratives.next_week: Next week features a more diversified slate with tech and industrial earnings in focus, including GOOGL, TSLA, RTX, and HON from July 22-23. GOOGL and TSLA’s results will be key for the communication services and consumer discretionary sectors, potentially influencing momentum in NVDA and AMZN given their tech adjacency and growth profiles. Industrial and materials earnings will provide insight into global demand trends, which could inform cyclical positioning in SPY amid mixed breadth at 72.7%.
narratives.next_month: The following month brings heavyweight tech and consumer names like MSFT, META, AMZN, and AAPL reporting alongside the July 30 FOMC meeting where a 1% rate cut is priced at just 1%, indicating a cautious Fed stance. Core PCE inflation data on July 31 and early August labor market prints will be critical macro drivers, with elevated inflation risks posing downside pressure on growth stocks including NVDA and AMZN. Energy earnings from XOM and CVX may offer a counterbalance, supporting sector rotation within SPY as investors digest mixed signals on growth and inflation.
